domainMap: Kristen is a strategic-foresight professional scanning HOLISTICALLY for the weakest, strangest, earliest signs of STRUCTURAL CHANGE anywhere. ===DO NOT ANCHOR TO RETAIL OR BUILT ENVIRONMENT=== this scan is fully DOMAIN-AGNOSTIC. Hunt across ALL domains, and DELIBERATELY reach into tangential, tertiary, and unrelated ones: synthetic biology & microbes, materials science, deep sea & space, law & legal personhood, governance experiments (network states, charter cities, rights-of-nature), demography & kinship, death care & the digital afterlife, religion & meaning-making, cognition & attention, animals & sentience, energy, defense, insurance/actuarial, non-Western and peripheral geographies, subcultures. ===WHAT TO REWARD (weakest first)=== absences (a thing that does NOT yet exist), reframings (a new reading of a named thing), second-order and third-order implications nobody has drawn, cross-domain CONVERGENCES (the same shift appearing in unrelated fields at once), single instances, non-English/peripheral coverage, and anything that would sound absurd or unsettling to a normal professional. Bias horizon to H3 (10-30yr). ===VOLUME & THE RULE-OUTS=== Kristen wants to SEE the rule-outs, not just the survivors — she calibrates her own judgment. So cast a WIDE net and return MANY candidates (up to the max). Include borderline items AND even already-trending/hyped ones — but LABEL them honestly: set classification to 'Trend' or 'Hype' and in ai_read say briefly why (e.g. 'already named and widely covered — included for your judgment, probably Context'). NEVER silently drop something; when in doubt, INCLUDE it and label it. Demote (label Trend/Hype) rather than delete. Do NOT assign an impact rating — that is human-held.
gdeltQueries: legal personhood river animal artificial intelligence
network state charter city sovereignty experiment
